projectDescription: A browser-native, WebGPU-powered MMD animation curve editor for creating and editing .vmd animation clips directly in the browser.
keyFeatures: WebGPU-powered real-time 3D rendering with IK and physics
Timeline with dopesheet and per-channel Bézier curve editor
PMX model and VMD animation import/export
Viewport bone picking and 3D transform gizmo
Per-channel Bézier interpolation editing (rotX, rotY, rotZ, tX, tY, tZ)
Track simplify (keyframe reduction) and clear operations
Keyboard shortcuts for playback, navigation, and editing
Bone list with grouped hierarchy and JP/EN labels
publicApiSurface: useStudioSelector(selector) — subscribe to a slice of document/selection state
useStudioActions() — stable actions: commit, replaceClip, setSelectedBone, setSelectedMorph, setSelectedMaterial, undo, redo
usePlaybackSelector(selector) — subscribe to transport state (currentFrame, playing)
usePlaybackFrameRef() — non-subscribing ref to live playhead frame
Studio — React provider for document/selection store
Playback — React provider for transport store
StudioState — clip, selectedBone, selectedMorph, selectedMaterial, selectedKeyframes, gizmoVisible, past, future
SelectedKeyframe — { bone?, morph?, frame, channel?, type: 'dope' | 'curve' }
BONE_GROUPS — predefined bone group mappings (Upper Body, Left/Right Arm, etc.)
ROT_CHANNELS / TRA_CHANNELS — per-channel definitions with key, label, color, get/set
quatToEuler(q) / eulerToQuat(ex, ey, ez) — quaternion ↔ Euler (YXZ, degrees)
EngineBridge — headless component for engine-coupled effects
File → New / Load PMX folder / Load VMD / Export VMD
